fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer audiologist license to texas, audiologist compact texas, texas audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Program
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
board.mailing_address: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation, P.O. Box 12157, Austin, TX 78711

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Texas.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for a new account and complete the Audiologist License application through the TDLR Online Licensing Services portal, paying the required application fee.

steps.title: Pass the Texas Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the online Texas State Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official university transcripts verifying a doctoral degree in audiology, official Praxis Audiology (5043) exam scores from ETS, and a completed Verification of Supervised Experience (VSE) Form to be sent directly to TDLR.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a criminal history background check by submitting fingerprints through the approved vendor as instructed by TDLR.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Texas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Texas
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Texas, submit your application and all required documentation through the TDLR Online Licensing Services portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the TDLR Online Licensing Services portal, select 'Register for a new account', create a User ID and Password, verify your email, and then log in to select 'Apply for a new license'.
